# WhatIs.site

> WhatIs.site provides clear, human-written explanations of concepts, ideas, and terms across technology, science, finance, philosophy, history, and more. Over 1,615 articles covering 25 categories.

## Categories

- [Technology](/category/technology): How the tools, systems, and innovations shaping our digital world actually work — from AI and blockchain to cloud computing and cybersecurity.
- [Science](/category/science): The natural world explained — physics, chemistry, biology, astronomy, and the scientific method itself.
- [Mathematics](/category/mathematics): Numbers, patterns, and logic. From basic arithmetic concepts to the abstract structures that underpin modern science.
- [Finance](/category/finance): Money, markets, and economic decisions. Understanding the financial systems that affect everyday life.
- [Philosophy](/category/philosophy): The big questions — existence, knowledge, ethics, logic, and the nature of mind. Ideas that have shaped how we think.
- [History](/category/history): People, events, and movements that shaped the world we live in. From ancient civilizations to the modern era.
- [Psychology](/category/psychology): How the human mind works — behavior, cognition, emotion, development, and the science behind why we do what we do.
- [Health & Wellness](/category/health-amp-wellness): The human body, medicine, nutrition, and mental health. Understanding what keeps us healthy and what happens when things go wrong.
- [Economics](/category/economics): How societies allocate resources, from supply and demand to macroeconomic policy and international trade.
- [Law](/category/law): Legal concepts, systems, and principles — from constitutional law to criminal justice and intellectual property.
- [Arts & Culture](/category/arts-amp-culture): Creative expression across mediums — visual art, literature, film, architecture, and the cultural movements that define eras.
- [Social Sciences](/category/social-sciences): The study of human society — sociology, anthropology, political science, and how people organize themselves.
- [Everyday Concepts](/category/everyday-concepts): Common terms and ideas you encounter daily, explained clearly so they actually make sense.
- [Business](/category/business): How organizations operate — management, strategy, entrepreneurship, and the mechanics of running a company.
- [Education](/category/education): Learning, teaching, and educational systems — from pedagogy and curricula to the philosophy of education.
- [Sports](/category/sports): Athletics, competition, and the games we play — rules, history, and the science of physical performance.
- [Music](/category/music): Sound, rhythm, and harmony. From music theory fundamentals to genres, instruments, and the physics of sound.
- [Food & Drink](/category/food-amp-drink): Culinary concepts, cooking techniques, ingredients, and the science behind what we eat and drink.
- [Language](/category/language): How human language works — linguistics, grammar, semantics, and the remarkable system we use to communicate.
- [Engineering](/category/engineering): Applying science to solve real problems — civil, mechanical, electrical, and software engineering concepts.
- [Biology & Medicine](/category/biology-amp-medicine): Life sciences and medical knowledge — from cellular biology to clinical medicine and public health.
- [Geography](/category/geography): The Earth's landscapes, climates, peoples, and places — physical and human geography explained.
- [Religion](/category/religion): World religions, spiritual practices, and belief systems — their histories, teachings, and cultural impact.
- [Politics](/category/politics): Government, power, and public policy — how political systems work and the ideologies that drive them.
- [Marketing](/category/marketing): How businesses reach and persuade audiences — branding, advertising, digital marketing, and consumer behavior.

## About

WhatIs.site is an educational content platform. Each article is structured with a clear definition in the opening paragraph, detailed sections, frequently asked questions, and citations to authoritative sources. Content is designed to be citation-worthy and quotable.
